content.problemStatement:
Evenvalue, the king of the even numbers has now set out to conquer the leaves of a rooted tree.

The tree has NNN nodes (numbered from 111 to NNN) and is rooted at node 111, which Evenvalue is currently on.

In one move, he can do the following -


If the current node UUU is a leaf, consider it conquered, choose XXX such that 1≤X≤K1 \le X \le K1≤X≤K, and move to the XthX^{th}Xth ancestor of UUU (if it exists).
Otherwise, move down to any one of the children of the current node.
Evenvalue wishes to conquer as many leaves as possible by some finite sequence of moves. Note that a leaf can only be conquered **at most** once. Please help him by counting the **maximum** number of leaves he can conquer.

Note:


XXX is said to be a child of YYY if XXX is adjacent to YYY and YYY lies on the unique shortest path connecting the root (which is 111) and XXX. In this case, YYY is said to be the parent or 1st1^{st}1st ancestor of XXX. The parent of 111 does not exist.
A leaf is a node which has no children.
The Xth(X>1)X^{th} (X > 1)Xth(X>1) ancestor of UUU is the parent of the (X−1)th(X - 1)^{th}(X−1)th ancestor of UUU.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the number of nodes and the maximum allowed jump from a leaf.
The next N−1N - 1N−1 lines describe the edges. The ithi^{th}ith of these N−1N - 1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers UiU_iUi​ and ViV_iVi​, denoting an edge between UiU_iUi​ and ViV_iVi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the maximum number of leaves Evenvalue can conquer using any finite sequence of moves.
constraints: The given set of edges describe a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Note that there are blank spaces in between test cases for Sample 222, which are not present in the actual tests. This is done to make the sample input clearer. You might need to remove these spaces when running your code on them.
Test case 111 : Evenvalue goes to 222, and then to 333 and thus conquers the leaf 333, which is also the only leaf. Thus he can only conquer atmost 111 leaf.

Explanation:
Test case 222 : Evenvalue first goes to 555, then jumps back to the 1st1^{st}1st ancestor of 555, i.e. 111, then he goes to 222, followed by 333 and 444. Thus he conquers both leaves of the tree.
Test case 333 : It can be shown that Evenvalue has a way to conquer leaves 666 and 444, but not all 333 simultaneously. Thus, the maximum is 222.
